title: Impulse — 2D Physics Engine
description: A 2D rigid-body physics engine built from scratch in TypeScript — GJK/EPA collision, a unified radius-aware narrowphase for circles, capsules and rounded polygons, and conservative-advancement continuous collision (CCD) so fast bodies never tunnel. The solver is a warm-started sequential-impulse method with split-impulse position correction AND an exact two-point block LCP (Box2D-Lite's four-case analysis) so wide stacks settle flat. It carries a from-scratch buoyancy & fluid-drag subsystem (real Archimedes lift ρ_fluid·A_submerged·(−g) at the centre of buoyancy, submerged area integrated in closed form against the waterline) and spatial queries (AABB region queries + a conservative-advancement convex shape-cast). New in v4 — a machine-builder's toolkit: the Box2D joint family is now complete with a pulley (length-ratio over two ground anchors), a gear joint (couples two revolute/prismatic joints by a ratio — gear trains & rack-and-pinion) and a motor joint (an overpowerable actuator that stalls under load); conveyor surfaces (a per-body tangentSpeed folded into the friction constraint so belts drag bodies up to speed); a radial-impulse / explosion field with distance falloff and static-geometry ray occlusion; and breakable joints that measure their own reaction load and snap past a force/torque budget. New in v5 — a second physics paradigm: from-scratch **soft bodies** via XPBD (extended position-based dynamics). Particles held by compliant distance, bending and area-preservation constraints, advanced with the step-size-independent 'small-steps' substep scheme, collide with every rigid shape through the engine's own core+radius math and exchange momentum two-ways — so a jelly shoves a crate and a falling crate dents a cloth hammock. Builders for pressurised blobs, pinnable cloth/membranes, ropes and lattice solids, a smooth jelly/cloth/rope renderer, and click-to-grab dragging. NEW in v6 — real-time **rigid-body fracture**: a brittle body shatters into convex **Voronoi shards** built by clipping its hull against one perpendicular-bisector half-plane per seed (a from-scratch power-free Voronoi, glass-style rings of seeds focused on the impact point). The split is a true rigid decomposition — every shard inherits the parent's velocity field vᵢ = v + ω×(cᵢ−C), so the shards' masses, **linear momentum** and **angular momentum about the parent's centre of mass** all sum back to the parent's exactly (the suite asserts Σm, Σp and ΣL = I·ω to 1e-9) and the object keeps moving as one until the contact solver prises the pieces apart. The world auto-shatters any brittle body whose strongest contact this step beats its toughness, with a generation cap that bounds the cascade; a click-to-shatter pointer tool and impact-spark renderer round it out. NEW in v7 — a third physics paradigm: an incompressible **Position-Based Fluid** (SPH, Macklin & Müller 2013) built from scratch — 2-D poly6/spiky kernels, a uniform-grid spatial hash for O(n) neighbour finding, the density-constraint Jacobi solve with CFM relaxation, XSPH viscosity, vorticity confinement and jet emitters, all **two-way coupled** to the rigid world through the same particle-vs-shape collision the soft engine uses (and a two-way position correction that gives genuine hydrostatic buoyancy — a light raft floats, a dense ingot sinks). A speed-shaded metaball renderer and a 💧 spray tool that paints water into any scene. Nine joint types, a dynamic AABB-tree broadphase, islands & sleeping, and a 46-scene interactive playground (Dam Break, Fountain, Communicating Vessels, Splash Pool, Waterwheel, Funnel, Water Sandbox, Glass Pane, Cannon & Wall, Shatter Tower, …) backed by a live 166-check correctness suite that re-derives every claim from an analytic reference (the SPH poly6 kernel integrates to 1; a settling fluid column stays incompressible at ~1.0 ρ₀ and conserves mass; Voronoi cells tile their parent exactly; fracture conserves mass, area & momentum; soft-body momentum conserved to ~1e-11; fully deterministic).
